items.title: Raya: Arsenal must play without fear to end title wait
items.summary: David Raya has called on Arsenal to play without fear as they look to win a first Premier League title in 22 years and a first piece of silverware since 2020.

items.title: VI Live: Lang in de basis bij CL-duel Galatasaray met Juventus
items.summary: Sparta Rotterdam en NEC maken dinsdagavond hun door de sneeuw geteisterde duel van afgelopen weekend in. Daarnaast gaat de tussenronde van de Champions League van start, met onder meer Real Madrid, Paris Saint-Germain, Borussia Dortmund en Juventus. Volg het in de VI Live.

items.title: Arteta suggests warmup change amid injuries
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has revealed Arsenal are assessing their warm-up routines after four players suffered fitness scares just before matches.

items.title: Luis Enrique slams 'worthless' Dembélé PSG take
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain coach Luis Enrique said he will not allow any player to be bigger than the club ahead of Tuesday's Champions League knockout phase playoff first leg against AS Monaco.

items.title: Klopp's agent: Man Utd, Chelsea made approach
items.summary: Jurgen Klopp's agent Marc Kosicke said his client had his pick of teams to manage after leaving Liverpool, including approaches from both the United States and England to take over as coach of their respective national teams.

items.title: Laporta in de aanval na nieuwe Barça-zeperd: 'Had gestaakt moeten worden'
items.summary: Joan Laporta is zijn verkiezingscampagne begonnen met een tirade richting het Spaanse scheidsrechtersgilde. De afgetreden voorzitter van Barcelona vindt het onbegrijpelijk dat de winnende goal van Girona niet werd afgekeurd en vindt zelfs dat de wedstrijd gestaakt had moeten worden. 'Het wordt een competitie tegen alles en iedereen.'

items.title: Van VELD naar VAN: Gertjan Verbeek over voetbal en huizen bouwen
items.summary: In deze serie met Mercedes-Benz rijden we door het hele land: van VELD naar VAN. We spreken oud-profvoetballers over hun verleden en hun carrièreswitch. Ooit reden ze met de spelersbus naar volle stadions. Nu rijden ze in hun eigen bedrijfswagen door het land tijdens hun tweede carrière. In deze aflevering is Gertjan Verbeek onze gast.

items.title: De unieke transformatie van PSV-goalie Schiks: 'Verder nog nooit gezien'
items.summary: Op achtjarige leeftijd kwam Schiks in 2012 als veldspeler de jeugdopleiding van de Eindhovenaren binnen. Jeugdtrainer Wolf kwam op het idee om Niek Schiks op doel te zetten, toen hij zag hoe de jonge Brabander voor trainingen voor de grap onder de lat ging staan en dat veel verdienstelijker deed dan verwacht. Op aandringen van PSV bleef hij er staan. Schiks richtte zich vanaf de Onder-13 definitief op het keepen.

items.title: I Am Maximus heads the weights for another Grand National
items.summary: I Am Maximus, winner of the Randox Grand National in 2024 and second last season, heads the weights for this year's Aintree spectacular.

items.title: Arteta admits Arsenal are looking at warm-up routines after ‘unusual’ spate of injuries
items.summary: Calafiori is latest player to pull up before kick-off ‘It’s happened four times this year … we have to learn’ Mikel Arteta has said he is looking at Arsenal’s warm-up routines “big time” as he seeks an explanation for why his title-chasing players keep pulling up before matches. Riccardo Calafiori dropped out of the lineup for the FA Cup tie against Wigan last Sunday after sustaining an injury in the warm-up, the second time it has happened to the player, and the fourth time in the season overall. Arteta said he was confident the Italian would be available against Wolves on Wednesday but said the club were “looking into” their pre-match habits. Continue reading...

items.title: Saka signs new five-year deal with Arsenal
items.summary: Bukayo Saka has signed a new five-year contract at Arsenal, Sky Sports News understands.

items.title: Bijna verdachte saaiheid wekt Manchester United tot leven
items.summary: Columnist Süleyman Öztürk over de grote waarde van onzichtbare spelers.

items.title: Sexton: Abuse will have marred Edogbo's Ireland debut
items.summary: Ireland coach Johnny Sexton says online abuse directed at debutant Edwin Edogbo will have marred a special day for him and his family.

items.title: Tudor says 'no time for excuses' about Spurs form
items.summary: New Tottenham Hotspur interim manager Igor Tudor says "nobody can accept" the club's current position.

items.title: Igor Tudor hopes courage and confidence can arrest Tottenham slide
items.summary: Interim manager: ‘There is no time to find excuses’ ‘The position of the club is one that nobody can accept’ Igor Tudor says there is “no time to find excuses” as he attempts to halt Tottenham’s Premier League slide. The 47-year-old former Juventus manager, brought in last week as the sacked Thomas Frank’s replacement until the end of the season, believes instilling the players with confidence is his most important task. “The team need, I believe, first of all, to get some confidence, to get some courage, but also, in same way, the concrete things in the pitch,” Tudor told Spursplay. “Of course, I’m coming here knowing that situation is not easy. There is no time to find excuses. What I said from the first day here, each of us need to give something more, something extra.” Continue reading...

items.title: Vítor Pereira insists he shares trust with ‘emotional’ Evangelos Marinakis
items.summary: ‘He trusts my work, I trust his personality’ Coach and owner have worked together at Olympiakos Vítor Pereira believes the trust he has with Nottingham Forest’s owner, Evangelos Marinakis, means he can survive the poisoned chalice of the City Ground job. The Portuguese has become Forest’s fourth manager of the season after Sean Dyche followed Nuno Espírito Santo and Ange Postecoglou out of the door as Marinakis acted in an attempt to avoid Premier League relegation. Continue reading...

items.title: Arsenal make Saka best-paid player with new five-year deal
items.summary: England winger Bukayo Saka commits his future to Premier League leaders Arsenal by signing a new five-year deal.

items.title: 'We created fire' - Forest boss Pereira on Marinakis reunion
items.summary: Nottingham Forest boss Vitor Pereira says "he trusts my work, I trust his personality" as he is reunited with Evangelos Marinakis.

items.title: ‘We had fun times’: Dennis Wise on the Crazy Gang, Chelsea and Como
items.summary: Wise remembers long throws with Vinnie Jones, training in a park with Gus Poyet and scoring in Europe for Millwall By The Coaches’ Voice As a young player I had been told a few times that I wasn’t quite good enough. Wimbledon manager Dave Bassett was the one who looked at me in a different way. He was the man who gave me that all-important opportunity. In terms of structuring a team, he was on the ball in everything he did. He was a long way in front of a lot of others, but because of the way he was, people looked at him in a different way. If he had been well-spoken and had what you might call an intelligent way about him, people would have looked at him differently. They would have said: “Wow, this guy is miles ahead.” Continue reading...

items.title: From Arsenal to FK Qarabag: Why every team left will, won't win the Champions League
items.summary: For each of the 24 remaining teams with a shot at the Champions League crown, let's talk about both why they could win the whole thing ... and why they probably won't.
